title: My Grief Is the Shape of My Love
subtitle: An affirmation for the grief that is still going and the love that keeps it alive
excerpt: A short affirmation for ongoing grief. Reframes persistent grief not as pathology or failure to move on, but as the natural consequence of having loved someone. The grief stays because the love was real.
sourceNotes: Written for homemade.education, drawing on the continuing-bonds model of grief, which holds that ongoing connection with the deceased is normal and healthy.

practice.whenToUse: Use when grief feels like something you should have moved past, or when you feel guilty for still being in it.

content.text: A short affirmation for grief that has been going a long time. Not a claim that the grief is easy, or that it should be. A reframe: the grief persists because the love was real. The size of the loss maps the size of what was there.
Say it when the grief is up. Three times.
My grief is the shape of my love. Both are real. Both are allowed to be here.

The affirmation does not ask the grief to leave. It asks you to understand why it is present. The grief is not proof of something wrong. It is the reverse image of the love that was there. One does not exist without the other.

Written for homemade.education, drawing on the continuing-bonds approach to grief, which holds that maintaining a connection with the person who has died is a normal and healthy part of how many people grieve, not a sign of pathology.
